description_long: This is a large, fantasy-inspired double-bladed axe with a dramatic, wing-like blade design. The blades are wide, symmetrical, and curve outward with pointed tips, giving the axe a menacing and ornamental appearance. The central shaft is robust and cylindrical, featuring a textured grip that appears to be wrapped, likely for improved handling. The axe head is mounted with a crossguard-like element, adding to its decorative and functional appeal. The overall color scheme is predominantly black with subtle metallic highlights, and the handle has a reddish-brown grip section. The construction suggests a heavy, durable weapon, possibly intended for ceremonial or display purposes as much as for practical use.
description: A large, double-bladed black axe with wing-shaped blades and a textured, reddish-brown handle grip. The design is ornate and imposing, with a robust central shaft and crossguard.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the wing-shaped double blades and the crossguard detail.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blades and the cylindrical handle.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying the symmetrical blade design and handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness of the axe and the straight alignment of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical handle, likely wood, wrapped with a textured reddish-brown grip for secure handling.
attribute_blade_design: Double-bladed, wing-shaped design with wide, outward-curving edges and pointed tips; symmetrical and ornamental with a crossguard.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with visible integration of the blade and handle.
